Verify against ESP32-S3 power requirements and create ngspice model. Candidates: TI TPS7A91, LT3042, or similar.

title: Integrate heuristics pipeline into CLI optimize command
description:
The heuristics subsystem (temper-600) is complete with 10 heuristics and a HeuristicPipeline orchestrator. However, it's not yet integrated into the CLI optimize command. The optimizer currently uses random initialization.

## Work needed
1. Import heuristics pipeline in cli.py
2. Add --heuristics/--no-heuristics flag (default: enabled)
3. Create PlacementContext from netlist/board/constraints
4. Run pipeline.run() to get initial placements
5. Convert PipelineResult to initial state for optimizer
6. Pass initial positions to train/train_multiphase

## Files
- cli.py: Add import and integration
- optimizer/__init__.py: May need to accept initial_positions parameter
- core/state.py: PlacementState.from_netlist_and_board may need init positions parameter

## Acceptance criteria
- temper-placer optimize uses heuristic initialization by default
- --no-heuristics flag reverts to random initialization  
- Log which heuristics ran and components placed

title: TDD: Ensure optimizer produces zero-DRC-error placements
description:
Create a TDD test suite that ensures optimized placements have zero DRC errors.

Current state:
- Perfect (hand-crafted) placements: 0 errors, 4 warnings (lib config)
- Optimized placements: 9-10 errors (clearance, shorting, solder mask bridge)

Root cause: Optimizer minimizes wirelength by pushing components close together, causing clearance violations.

TDD approach:
1. test_optimized_placement_zero_drc_errors - MUST pass
2. test_optimizer_respects_clearance_rules - Add clearance loss
3. test_convergence_maintains_drc_compliance - Check throughout optimization

Implementation options:
a) Add ClearanceLoss that penalizes components closer than DRC minimum
b) Increase overlap penalty weight to discourage close placement
c) Add hard constraint for minimum component spacing

Success criteria: An optimized placement (400 epochs) has 0 DRC errors.

title: Firmware HAL Abstraction and TDD Infrastructure
description:
Implement Hardware Abstraction Layer (HAL) and Test-Driven Development infrastructure for the Temper induction cooker firmware. This enables unit testing of all control logic (state machine, PID, safety, pan detection, PLL) without hardware dependencies.

**Goals:**
1. Abstract all hardware access through HAL interfaces (GPIO, ADC, PWM, SPI, Timer)
2. Create mock implementations for host-based testing
3. Implement comprehensive unit tests using ESP-IDF Unity framework
4. Verify firmware behavior against design specifications

**Key Specifications to Test Against:**
- OCP threshold: 50A ±5A (2.5V at comparator)
- OVP threshold: 390V (3.55V after divider)
- Thermal shutdown: 85°C IGBT junction
- Watchdog timeout: 1.6s
- Temperature precision: ±0.5°C
- Switching frequency: 38-50 kHz
- Dead-time: 500ns minimum
- Pan detection: valid 3-15A, no-pan >20A, small object <3A

**Deliverables:**
- HAL interface headers in firmware/components/hal/include/
- Mock implementations in firmware/components/hal/mock/
- ESP32 implementations in firmware/components/hal/esp32/
- Test suite in firmware/test/
- All tests passing against design specifications

title: Define HAL interface headers
description:
Create HAL interface header files that abstract all hardware access:

**Files to create:**
- hal_types.h - Common types (hal_status_t, hal_pin_t, etc.)
- hal_gpio.h - GPIO operations (init, set_level, get_level, set_interrupt)
- hal_adc.h - ADC operations (init, read, read_voltage, calibrate)
- hal_pwm.h - PWM operations (init, set_freq, set_duty, set_dead_time, start/stop)
- hal_spi.h - SPI operations (init, transfer, read_reg, write_reg)
- hal_timer.h - Timer operations (init, start, stop, get_count, set_callback)

**Design pattern:**
- Use function pointer structs for runtime binding
- Support dependency injection for testing
- Include error handling with hal_status_t return codes
- Document thread safety requirements

**Location:** firmware/components/hal/include/

title: Integration validation and documentation
description:
Final integration testing and documentation:

**Integration tests:**
1. Full startup sequence (INIT → IDLE → PAN_DETECT → HEATING)
2. Normal operation cycle with temperature control
3. Fault injection and recovery scenarios
4. Power cycle behavior
5. Stress testing (rapid state changes, edge cases)

**Documentation:**
1. Update firmware README with test instructions
2. Document HAL API usage patterns
3. Add inline documentation to all headers
4. Create test coverage report
5. Document known limitations

**Verification checklist:**
- [ ] All unit tests pass on host
- [ ] All unit tests pass on target (ESP32)
- [ ] Code coverage >80% for critical paths
- [ ] No memory leaks (valgrind on host)
- [ ] Static analysis clean (cppcheck)
- [ ] All failure modes from design docs have tests

**Test file:** firmware/test/test_integration.c

**Expected outcome:** Fully tested, documented firmware ready for hardware bring-up

title: test_integration.c - Full startup sequence test
description: Test full startup: INIT → IDLE → PAN_DETECT → HEATING. Verify each state transition fires correctly. Mock HAL provides valid readings. Test with valid pan detection. File: firmware/test/test_integration.c

title: Create test_integration.c file with Unity test infrastructure
description: Create new file firmware/test/test_integration.c with: Unity includes, test_common.h include, test runner function run_integration_tests(), and main test entry point. File should follow pattern from existing test files like test_state_machine.c.

title: Create test_main_integration.c entry point
description: Create firmware/test/test_main_integration.c following pattern of test_main_state_machine.c. Include Unity setup, call run_integration_tests(), return UnityEnd().

title: Add test_integration target to CMakeLists.txt
description: Update firmware/test/CMakeLists.txt to add test_integration executable. Include test_integration.c, test_main_integration.c, state_machine_stubs.c, unity/unity.c. Link against mock HAL and control libraries.

title: Implement test_startup_init_to_idle - verify INIT→IDLE on self-test pass
description: Test case: After state_machine_init(), call state_machine_update(). Verify state transitions from STATE_INIT to STATE_IDLE. Use mock_sm_reset() to set up clean state. Assert state == STATE_IDLE and fault == FAULT_NONE.

title: Implement test_startup_idle_to_pan_detect - verify IDLE→PAN_DET on start button
description: Test case: From IDLE state, set target temp, press start button via mock_sm_press_button(BUTTON_START), advance time, call update. Verify state == STATE_PAN_DET. Verify display shows 'PLACE PAN' message.

title: Implement test_startup_pan_detect_to_preheat - verify PAN_DET→PREHEAT on pan present
description: Test case: From PAN_DET state, set mock_sm_set_pan_status(MOCK_PAN_PRESENT), iterate updates to build confidence (PAN_CONFIDENCE_REQUIRED=3). Verify state == STATE_PREHEAT. Verify PLL is enabled via mock_sm_get_pll_enabled().

title: Implement test_startup_preheat_to_heating - verify PREHEAT→HEATING near target temp
description: Test case: From PREHEAT state, set mock_sm_set_pan_temperature() to within 10°C of target, call update. Verify state == STATE_HEATING. Test uses target temp 100°C, measurement 92°C.

title: Implement test_full_startup_sequence - end-to-end INIT→IDLE→PAN_DET→PREHEAT→HEATING
description: Integration test combining all startup transitions in sequence: Initialize, pass self-test, press start, detect pan, approach target temp. Verify each intermediate state. Verify power level increases during heating. Comprehensive 'happy path' test.

title: test_integration.c - Normal operation cycle test
description: Test normal heating cycle with temperature control. Start heating, reach setpoint, maintain temperature, stop. Verify PID controller regulates correctly. Mock temperature sensor readings.

title: Implement test_operation_temperature_regulation - PID maintains setpoint
description: Test case: From HEATING state, simulate gradual temperature approach to setpoint. Call state_machine_update() repeatedly while advancing time. Verify temperature stays within ±0.5°C of target (TEMP_PRECISION_C). Verify power output adjusts (decreases as temp approaches target).

title: Implement test_operation_power_level_adjustment - verify power responds to temp delta
description: Test case: In HEATING state, vary mock temperature (below setpoint, at setpoint, above setpoint). Verify mock_sm_get_power_level() returns appropriate values: high power when cold, low/zero power when at/above target. Test PID proportional response.

title: Implement test_operation_timer_countdown - timer expires and transitions to COOLDOWN
description: Test case: In HEATING state, call state_machine_set_timer(true, 5000) for 5 second timer. Advance time 5500ms via mock_sm_advance_time(). Verify state transitions to STATE_COOLDOWN. Verify 'COMPLETE' message displayed.

title: Implement test_operation_manual_stop - stop button transitions to COOLDOWN
description: Test case: In HEATING state at steady temperature, press stop button via mock_sm_press_button(BUTTON_STOP). Call update. Verify state == STATE_COOLDOWN. Verify power == 0. Verify PLL disabled.

title: Implement test_operation_cooldown_to_idle - verify COOLDOWN→IDLE when cool
description: Test case: In COOLDOWN state, set mock_sm_set_heatsink_temperature(45.0f) below safe idle threshold (50°C). Call update. Verify state == STATE_IDLE. Verify LED shows steady green pattern.

title: Implement test_full_heating_cycle - complete cycle from start to stop to idle
description: Full integration test: IDLE→PAN_DET→PREHEAT→HEATING (temp regulation)→COOLDOWN→IDLE. Simulate realistic temperature curve. Verify watchdog fed throughout. Verify power profile matches expectations. End-to-end 'happy path' for normal cooking operation.

title: test_integration.c - Fault injection tests
description: Test fault injection and recovery: OCP fault via mock ADC, OVP fault, thermal fault via mock SPI, watchdog timeout. Verify proper transition to FAULT state and recovery after reset.

title: Implement test_fault_ocp_during_heating - over-current fault via mock ADC
description: Test case: In HEATING state, set mock_sm_set_dc_bus_current(55.0f) above OCP threshold (50A±5A). Call update. Verify state == STATE_FAULT. Verify fault == FAULT_OVER_CURRENT. Verify power immediately disabled. Verify PWM disabled. Verify fault logged to EEPROM.

title: Implement test_fault_reset_rejected_while_active - cannot clear active fault
description: Test case: Trigger over-temp fault. While fault condition still active (temp > 100°C), press reset button. Verify state remains STATE_FAULT. Verify fault not cleared. Safety critical - must not allow restart while unsafe.

title: Implement test_fault_ovp_during_heating - over-voltage fault
description: Test case: In HEATING state, inject DC bus voltage above OVP threshold (390V). Call update. Verify state == STATE_FAULT. Verify fault == FAULT_OVER_VOLTAGE. Verify hardware shutdown triggered via mock_sm_get_trigger_shutdown_count().

title: Implement test_fault_thermal_igbt - IGBT over-temperature via mock SPI
description: Test case: In HEATING state, set mock_sm_set_heatsink_temperature(105.0f) above 100°C limit. Call update. Verify state == STATE_FAULT. Verify fault == FAULT_OVER_TEMP. Test thermal shutdown at 85°C IGBT junction threshold as per specs.

title: Implement test_fault_rtd_open - temperature sensor open circuit
description: Test case: In HEATING state, set mock_sm_set_rtd_resistance(15000.0f) above 10kΩ (open circuit). Call update. Verify state == STATE_FAULT. Verify fault == FAULT_PROBE_OPEN. Verify safe shutdown (power off, PLL disabled).

title: Implement test_fault_rtd_short - temperature sensor short circuit
description: Test case: In HEATING state, set mock_sm_set_rtd_resistance(5.0f) below 10Ω (short circuit). Call update. Verify state == STATE_FAULT. Verify fault == FAULT_PROBE_SHORT.

title: Implement test_fault_fan_failure - cooling fan stopped
description: Test case: In HEATING state, set mock_sm_set_fan_running(false). Call update. Verify state == STATE_FAULT. Verify fault == FAULT_FAN_FAILURE. Critical safety fault - must stop heating immediately.

title: Implement test_fault_thermal_runaway - temperature exceeds target by margin
description: Test case: Set target temp 100°C. In HEATING state, set mock_sm_set_pan_temperature(115.0f) exceeding target+10°C. Call update. Verify state == STATE_FAULT. Verify fault == FAULT_THERMAL_RUNAWAY. Tests runaway detection safety.

title: Implement test_fault_watchdog_timeout - no reset causes shutdown
description: Test case: Simulate watchdog not being fed by advancing time past 1600ms (TPS3823-33 timeout) without calling update. Verify hardware shutdown triggered. This tests the external watchdog behavior expectation.

title: Implement test_fault_recovery_after_condition_cleared - reset from FAULT state
description: Test case: Trigger over-temp fault. Clear condition (set heatsink temp to 65°C < 70°C threshold). Press reset button. Verify state transitions to STATE_INIT. Verify fault == FAULT_NONE. Verify system can restart normally.

title: test_integration.c - Power cycle behavior test
description: Test power cycle scenarios: graceful shutdown, unexpected power loss (state persistence), startup after fault. Verify system initializes correctly from various states.

title: test_integration.c - Stress testing
description: Test edge cases: rapid state changes, concurrent events, boundary conditions, max/min values. Verify no race conditions or undefined behavior under stress.

title: Update firmware README with test instructions
description: Document how to run tests: host-based testing with mock HAL, target testing on ESP32. Include build commands, test runner usage, interpreting results. File: firmware/README.md

title: Document HAL API usage patterns
description: Add usage examples and patterns to HAL headers. Show how to initialize each HAL, common use cases, error handling. Include code snippets in header comments.

title: Run valgrind memory leak check on host tests
description: Run all tests under valgrind on host. Fix any memory leaks found. Document clean valgrind output. Command: valgrind --leak-check=full ./test_runner

title: Run cppcheck static analysis
description: Run cppcheck on firmware code. Fix all warnings and errors. Command: cppcheck --enable=all firmware/. Document clean output.

title: Implement mock HAL for testing
description:
Create mock implementations of all HAL interfaces for host-based testing:

**Files to create:**
- mock_gpio.c - Simulated GPIO with state tracking
- mock_adc.c - Programmable ADC values for test scenarios
- mock_pwm.c - PWM state capture and verification
- mock_spi.c - SPI transaction recording and response injection
- mock_timer.c - Controllable time simulation

**Mock capabilities:**
- Inject specific values (e.g., ADC reads return OCP threshold)
- Record all calls for verification
- Simulate timing behavior
- Support fault injection (return errors)
- Thread-safe for concurrent test execution

**Test helper functions:**
- mock_gpio_set_input_level() - Set what get_level() returns
- mock_adc_set_next_value() - Queue ADC readings
- mock_pwm_get_state() - Verify PWM configuration
- mock_timer_advance() - Control time progression

**Location:** firmware/components/hal/mock/

title: Set up test infrastructure with Unity
description:
Configure ESP-IDF Unity test framework for host-based testing:

**Setup tasks:**
1. Create firmware/test/ directory structure
2. Configure CMakeLists.txt for test builds
3. Set up Unity test runner
4. Create test helper utilities
5. Configure CI-friendly test execution

**CMake configuration:**
- Support both target (ESP32) and host builds
- Link mock HAL for host builds, real HAL for target
- Generate test coverage reports
- Support parallel test execution

**Test utilities to create:**
- test_common.h - Shared test macros and helpers
- test_fixtures.c - Common setup/teardown patterns
- assertion_helpers.c - Domain-specific assertions (e.g., ASSERT_TEMP_WITHIN)

**Commands:**

- idf.py -T test flash monitor - Run on target
- ./build/test_runner - Run host tests directly

**Reference:** ESP-IDF Unity docs at docs.espressif.com

title: State machine tests and fixes
description:
Write comprehensive tests for state_machine.c and fix any failures:

**States to test:**
- STATE_INIT → STATE_IDLE (on successful init)
- STATE_IDLE → STATE_PAN_DETECT (on start command)
- STATE_PAN_DETECT → STATE_HEATING (on valid pan)
- STATE_PAN_DETECT → STATE_IDLE (on no pan/timeout)
- STATE_HEATING → STATE_IDLE (on stop command)
- STATE_HEATING → STATE_FAULT (on any fault)
- STATE_FAULT → STATE_IDLE (on reset after fault cleared)

**Failure modes to test:**
1. Invalid state transitions should be rejected
2. Stuck in FAULT should require explicit reset
3. Race conditions between concurrent events
4. Timeout handling in PAN_DETECT state
5. Entry/exit actions execute correctly
6. State persistence across power cycles (if applicable)

**Test file:** firmware/test/test_state_machine.c

**Expected test count:** ~15-20 tests

title: PID controller tests and fixes
description:
Write comprehensive tests for pid_controller.c and fix any failures:

**Functionality to test:**
1. Basic P, I, D term calculations
2. Setpoint tracking (step response)
3. Disturbance rejection
4. Anti-windup behavior (integral clamping)
5. Derivative kick prevention on setpoint change
6. Output clamping to 0-100% range
7. Zero dt handling (no division by zero)
8. Reset/initialize behavior

**Test scenarios:**
- Step input: verify settling time and overshoot
- Steady state: verify zero steady-state error
- Windup: saturate output, verify integral doesn't explode
- Derivative: change setpoint, verify no spike
- Edge cases: dt=0, extreme gains, overflow conditions

**Specifications from design:**
- Temperature precision: ±0.5°C
- Output range: 0-100% duty cycle
- Update rate: 10Hz typical

**Test file:** firmware/test/test_pid_controller.c

**Expected test count:** ~12-15 tests

title: Safety interlock tests and fixes
description:
Write comprehensive tests for safety_interlock.c and fix any failures:

**Safety conditions to test:**
1. OCP (Over-Current Protection): Trip at 50A ±5A (2.5V comparator)
2. OVP (Over-Voltage Protection): Trip at 390V (3.55V after divider)
3. Thermal shutdown: Trip at 85°C IGBT junction
4. Watchdog timeout: Trip after 1.6s without kick
5. Interlock chain: Door/cover sensors
6. Emergency stop: Immediate shutdown

**Failure modes to test:**
1. Fault latching - fault stays set until explicit reset
2. Reset rejection - cannot reset while fault condition active
3. Multiple faults - all faults detected, most severe reported
4. Startup glitch immunity - ignore transients <10µs
5. Fault response time - shutdown within 1µs for OCP
6. Fault logging - timestamp and type recorded

**Test scenarios:**
- Inject OCP via mock ADC, verify shutdown
- Inject OVP via mock ADC, verify shutdown
- Inject thermal via mock SPI (MAX31865), verify shutdown
- Starve watchdog, verify timeout fault
- Clear fault condition, verify reset works
- Try reset with fault active, verify rejection

**Test file:** firmware/test/test_safety_interlock.c

**Expected test count:** ~20-25 tests

title: Pan detection tests and fixes
description:
Write comprehensive tests for pan_detection.c and fix any failures:

**Pan detection thresholds (from RESONANT_TANK_DESIGN.md):**
- Valid pan: 3-15A coil current
- No pan: >20A (resonant tank unloaded)
- Small object: <3A (insufficient coupling)
- Ferromagnetic: Look for expected impedance pattern

**Functionality to test:**
1. Valid pan detection (3-15A range)
2. No pan detection (>20A)
3. Small object rejection (<3A)
4. Debouncing - single glitch doesn't trigger
5. Hysteresis - no oscillation at boundaries
6. Pan removal during heating - proper shutdown
7. Calibration support - adjustable thresholds

**Failure modes to test:**
1. No debouncing causing false triggers
2. Hardcoded thresholds vs calibratable
3. Missing hysteresis causing oscillation
4. Not distinguishing pan removal vs small object
5. Timeout if no decision reached

**Test scenarios:**
- Inject 10A reading → expect VALID_PAN
- Inject 25A reading → expect NO_PAN
- Inject 1A reading → expect SMALL_OBJECT
- Inject alternating 14A/16A → verify no oscillation (hysteresis)
- Inject single 25A spike then 10A → verify debounce ignores spike

**Test file:** firmware/test/test_pan_detection.c

**Expected test count:** ~10-15 tests

title: PLL/ZVS tracking tests and fixes
description:
Write comprehensive tests for pll_tracking.c and fix any failures:

**PLL specifications:**
- Frequency range: 38-50 kHz
- Phase target: 0° (ZVS condition)
- Lock detection: phase error <5° for 10 cycles
- Unlock detection: phase error >30° for 5 cycles

**Functionality to test:**
1. Phase detection from ZCD signal
2. Frequency adjustment algorithm
3. Lock acquisition from startup
4. Lock maintenance during load changes
5. Unlock detection and recovery
6. Frequency limits enforcement
7. Soft start behavior

**Failure modes to test:**
1. Loss of lock not detected
2. Phase wrapping at ±180° boundary
3. Frequency exceeds safe limits
4. No soft recovery from unlock
5. Oscillation around lock point
6. Incorrect phase calculation

**Test scenarios:**
- Start at 40kHz, inject ZCD at 42kHz → verify tracking
- Inject phase lead → verify frequency increase
- Inject phase lag → verify frequency decrease  
- Inject sudden phase jump → verify unlock detection
- Test at frequency limits → verify clamping
- Remove ZCD signal → verify fault handling

**Test file:** firmware/test/test_pll_tracking.c

**Expected test count:** ~12-15 tests

title: ESP32 HAL implementations
description:
Create real ESP32 implementations of all HAL interfaces:

**Files to create:**
- esp32_gpio.c - ESP-IDF gpio driver wrapper
- esp32_adc.c - ESP-IDF ADC driver with calibration
- esp32_pwm.c - MCPWM driver for complementary PWM with dead-time
- esp32_spi.c - ESP-IDF SPI master driver
- esp32_timer.c - ESP-IDF timer/counter driver

**ESP32-S3 specific considerations:**
- Use MCPWM for gate driver PWM (hardware dead-time)
- Use ADC continuous mode for current sensing
- Use SPI DMA for MAX31865 temperature reads
- Configure GPIO matrix for signal routing
- Handle FreeRTOS threading safely

**Pin assignments (from schematic):**
- PWM_HI: GPIO X (to UCC21550 INA)
- PWM_LO: GPIO Y (to UCC21550 INB)  
- ADC_CURRENT: ADC1_CH0
- ADC_VOLTAGE: ADC1_CH1
- SPI_CLK/MOSI/MISO/CS: GPIO for MAX31865
- ZCD_INPUT: GPIO with interrupt

**Location:** firmware/components/hal/esp32/

**Note:** This task can proceed in parallel with testing since tests use mock HAL

title: Create esp32/ directory and CMakeLists.txt for ESP32 HAL
description:
Create firmware/components/hal/esp32/ directory with CMakeLists.txt that:
- Includes ESP-IDF component dependencies (driver, esp_adc, esp_timer)

title: Implement esp32_gpio.c - ESP-IDF GPIO wrapper
description:
Implement ESP32 GPIO HAL using ESP-IDF gpio driver:

**Functions to implement:**
- esp32_gpio_init() - gpio_config() with pull-up/pull-down
- esp32_gpio_set_level() - gpio_set_level()

title: Implement esp32_adc.c - ESP-IDF ADC with calibration
description:
Implement ESP32 ADC HAL using ESP-IDF ADC driver:

**Functions to implement:**
- esp32_adc_init() - adc_oneshot_new_unit() + adc_oneshot_config_channel()
- esp32_adc_read_raw() - adc_oneshot_read()
- esp32_adc_read_voltage() - adc_cali_raw_to_voltage()
- esp32_adc_calibrate() - adc_cali_create_scheme_curve_fitting()
- esp32_adc_start_continuous() - adc_continuous_new_handle() + adc_continuous_start()
- esp32_adc_stop_continuous() - adc_continuous_stop()
- esp32_adc_get_latest() - adc_continuous_read() with latest value
- esp32_adc_deinit() - adc_oneshot_del_unit()

**ESP-IDF APIs (v5.x):**
- #include "esp_adc/adc_oneshot.h"
- #include "esp_adc/adc_cali.h"
- #include "esp_adc/adc_cali_scheme.h"
- #include "esp_adc/adc_continuous.h"

title: temper-placer: Numerical stability and correctness fixes
description: Critical fixes for numerical stability issues and correctness bugs identified during code review. These issues can cause silent failures (NaN propagation), training explosions (overflow), or data corruption (coordinate asymmetry). Must be fixed before the optimizer can be trusted for production use.

title: Add NaN/Inf detection in training loop
description:
**Problem:** train.py lines 398 and 627 convert loss to float without checking for NaN/Inf. If any loss function returns NaN, training continues blindly producing garbage results.

**Current code:**
```python
loss_value = float(loss)  # No check for NaN/Inf!
```

**Fix needed:**
```python
loss_value = float(loss)
if not jnp.isfinite(loss_value):
    raise RuntimeError(f'Non-finite loss at epoch {epoch}: {loss_value}')
```

Also add component-level loss logging to identify WHICH loss function produced NaN.

**Files:** temper-placer/src/temper_placer/optimizer/train.py lines 398, 627
**Risk:** HIGH - Silent garbage output, wasted compute time

title: Fix LogSumExp overflow in wirelength loss
description:
**Problem:** wirelength.py line 145 uses `large_val = 1e10` for masking invalid connections. With alpha=10 (default), this computes `exp(alpha * 1e10) = exp(1e11) = Inf`, even inside logsumexp.

**Current code:**
```python
large_val = 1e10  # Used for masking
x_max = jax.nn.logsumexp(self.alpha * x_for_max, axis=1) / self.alpha
```

**Fix options:**
1. Reduce `large_val` to `1e6` (still huge but won't overflow)
2. Use `-jnp.inf` for proper masking (logsumexp handles -inf correctly)
3. Use `jnp.where` to exclude masked values before logsumexp

**Files:** temper-placer/src/temper_placer/losses/wirelength.py line 145
**Risk:** HIGH - Training explodes with Inf values

title: Fix Gumbel-Softmax overflow at low temperature
description:
**Problem:** transform.py lines 437-443 compute Gumbel-Softmax without clamping. At temperature=0.1, logits are divided by 0.1 (multiplied by 10). Combined with Gumbel noise (can reach ~7), values can reach 70+, causing `exp(70) ≈ 1e30` overflow.

**Current code:**
```python
noisy_logits = (logits + gumbel_noise) / temperature  # temperature can be 0.1
soft_samples = jax.nn.softmax(noisy_logits, axis=-1)
```

**Fix options:**
1. Clamp noisy_logits before softmax: `jnp.clip(noisy_logits, -50, 50)`
2. Enforce temperature floor: `temperature = max(temperature, 0.5)`
3. Use numerically stable softmax with max subtraction (JAX may do this already, verify)

**Files:** temper-placer/src/temper_placer/geometry/transform.py lines 437-443
**Risk:** HIGH - Training explodes at low temperatures during curriculum

title: Fix coordinate system asymmetry in KiCad round-trip
description:
**Problem:** Import and export handle board origin asymmetrically, causing DATA CORRUPTION on round-trip.

**Import (kicad_parser.py):** Takes raw KiCad coordinates `(100, 80)`
**Export (kicad_writer.py):** Adds origin offset `x + origin[0]`

If board has `origin = (50, 30)`, a component at `(100, 80)` becomes `(150, 110)` after round-trip - **WRONG!**

**Test to verify bug:**
```python
# Parse PCB, immediately write it back, parse again
# Coordinates should match - currently they don't
```

**Fix options:**
1. Subtract origin on import, add on export (normalize to origin-relative)
2. Don't modify coordinates at all (pass through as-is)
3. Store original origin and use consistently

**Files:** 
- temper-placer/src/temper_placer/io/kicad_parser.py
- temper-placer/src/temper_placer/io/kicad_writer.py
**Risk:** HIGH - Exported PCB files have wrong component positions

title: Add chunking to SpreadLoss for O(n²) mitigation
description:
**Problem:** regularization.py line 51 creates full pairwise distance matrix without chunking:

```python
diff = positions[:, None, :] - positions[None, :, :]  # Creates (N, N, 2) tensor
```

OverlapLoss has chunking logic, but SpreadLoss doesn't. For 100 components this creates 10,000 pairs every forward pass. For 200 components, 40,000 pairs.

**Fix:** Port chunking pattern from OverlapLoss to SpreadLoss:
```python
# Process in chunks of 32 components
for i in range(0, n, chunk_size):
    for j in range(0, n, chunk_size):
        # Compute pairwise distances for chunk
```

**Files:** temper-placer/src/temper_placer/losses/regularization.py line 51
**Risk:** MEDIUM - Slow training, memory pressure on large boards

title: Fix or document skipped integration tests
description:
**Problem:** Two critical tests in test_integration.py are SKIPPED, hiding known bugs:

1. `test_parse_main_schematic` - 'Schematic parsing needs refinement for hierarchical designs'
2. `test_write_and_read_minimal_pcb` - 'KiCad parser reference extraction needs refinement'

These indicate known issues in the KiCad round-trip pipeline that should either be:
1. Fixed and tests unskipped
2. Documented as known limitations with bd issues tracking the fix

**Action items:**
- Investigate why each test is skipped
- Either fix the underlying issue OR create tracking issues
- If known limitation, document in README

**Files:** temper-placer/tests/io/test_integration.py
**Risk:** MEDIUM - Unknown failures in production use

title: Add defensive edge-case tests
description:
**Problem:** No tests verify behavior for edge cases that will occur in real usage:

1. **Empty netlist (0 components)** - Likely causes array shape errors
2. **Single component** - May break pairwise loss functions
3. **All components fixed** - No learnable parameters, optimizer may crash
4. **Component larger than board** - Boundary loss undefined behavior
5. **All components in same net** - Wirelength edge case
6. **No nets defined** - Wirelength returns what?

**Tests to add:**
```python
def test_empty_netlist():
    # Should raise clear error or return no-op

def test_single_component():
    # Should work, all pairwise losses return 0

def test_all_fixed_components():
    # Should warn and skip optimization

def test_oversized_component():
    # Should raise error or clamp to board
```

**Files:** temper-placer/tests/ (new test file: test_edge_cases.py)
**Risk:** MEDIUM - Cryptic errors when users hit edge cases

title: Handle non-90° rotations gracefully
description:
**Problem:** kicad_parser.py line 236 silently truncates non-90° rotations:

```python
rot = int(fp.position.angle / 90) % 4  # 45° becomes 0!
```

Components with 45° or arbitrary rotations lose their rotation information.

**Options:**
1. **Warn on import** - Log warning when non-90° rotation detected
2. **Preserve original** - Store original angle, only use 90° buckets for optimization
3. **Reject** - Raise error if non-90° rotations found (strict mode)

**Recommendation:** Option 2 - preserve original angle in component metadata, use nearest 90° for optimization, restore original offset on export.

**Files:** temper-placer/src/temper_placer/io/kicad_parser.py line 236
**Risk:** LOW - Most PCB components use 90° rotations, but some don't

description:

- Cover key parsing features: footprints, nets, zones, layers
- Self-contained, no external dependencies
- Version-controlled with the project

**Files:** temper-placer/tests/fixtures/ (new directory)
**Risk:** LOW - Tests work locally but fail on CI

title: Epic: Resonant Tank and Pan Load Simulation
description:
Design and simulate the resonant LC tank and pan load equivalent circuit.

THEORY:
- Series LC resonance: f_r = 1/(2π√(LC))
- Target frequency: 30-40kHz
- Coil inductance: ~50µH (with pan coupled)
- Resonant capacitor: ~400nF

KEY CHALLENGE - VARIABLE LOAD:
Pan coupling varies based on material, size, and air gap, causing:
- Inductance shift (affecting resonant frequency)
- Reflected resistance change (affecting power delivery)
- Coupling coefficient variation (k = 0.2 to 0.6 typical)

DELIVERABLES:
1. simulation/models/pan_load.sub - Pan equivalent circuit model
2. sim_24_resonant_tank_ac.cir - Frequency sweep analysis
3. sim_25_zvs_verification.cir - Soft-switching conditions
4. sim_26_full_power_stage.cir - Half-bridge + resonant tank
5. RESONANT_TANK_DESIGN.md - Complete design document

Verify dead-time accuracy, propagation delay matching between channels A/B (33ns typical), delay variation with temperature/voltage, and jitter characteristics. Confirm dead-time > t_fall(IGBT) + t_delay + margin per calculation in compatibility report.

Verify end-to-end timing: ESP32 PWM generation → RC filter → gate driver logic → output stage → gate charge delivery. Measure total propagation delay, verify UVLO protection, test fault disable response time (48ns spec). Include realistic power supply sequencing.

title: Refine BoundaryLoss for Strict <10.0 Violation
description:
Achieve strict <10.0 boundary violation on all test PCBs.

## Background
- Current threshold relaxed to 20.0 to pass piantor_right (temper-a63)
- Long-term goal is <10.0 for production-quality placements
- piantor_right final boundary loss was 54.0 → need ~5x improvement

## Target Boards
| PCB | Current Boundary | Target | Gap |
|-----|------------------|--------|-----|
| piantor_left | ~8.0 | <10.0 | ✅ OK |
| piantor_right | 54.0 | <10.0 | 5.4x |
| bitaxe_ultra | ~5.0 | <10.0 | ✅ OK |
| rp2040_designguide | ~12.0 | <10.0 | 1.2x |

## Root Cause Analysis

### Why piantor_right has high boundary loss:
1. **Board shape**: Narrow, elongated keyboard PCB
2. **Component density**: 36 components in constrained space
3. **Competing objectives**: Wirelength pulls components toward center, boundary pushes away

### Current BoundaryLoss behavior:
```python
# From losses/boundary.py
excess = jax.nn.relu(distance_outside_board)
penalty = weight * excess ** 2
```
- Quadratic penalty kicks in only when component is outside
- No "soft" pressure to stay away from edges
- Weight may be too low relative to other losses

## Approaches to Test

### Approach 1: Increase BoundaryLoss Weight
Current: weight=200, try weight=500-1000
```yaml
losses:
  boundary:
    weight: 500  # was 200
```
**Risk**: May push components too far from edge, hurt wirelength

### Approach 2: Add Margin Buffer
Penalize being within X mm of edge, not just outside:
```python
margin = 2.0  # mm
excess = jax.nn.relu(margin - distance_to_edge)
```
**Benefit**: Smoother gradient, prevents edge-crowding

### Approach 3: Annealing Schedule
Start with low boundary weight, ramp up in final epochs:
```python
boundary_weight = base_weight * min(1.0, epoch / anneal_epochs)
# e.g., 200 → 500 over epochs 1500-2000
```
**Benefit**: Let wirelength converge first, then tighten boundary

### Approach 4: Per-Component Margin
Larger components need more margin from edge:
```python
margin = max(2.0, component.width / 2)
```

### Approach 5: Initialization Closer to Center
Start components away from edges (see temper-1my.7 spectral init)

## Experiment Protocol

### Phase 1: Weight Tuning
```bash
for weight in 200 300 500 750 1000; do
  temper-placer optimize --boundary-weight $weight piantor_right.kicad_pcb
done
```
Record: boundary loss, wirelength, overlap, runtime

### Phase 2: Margin Buffer
Implement margin buffer, test with margin={1.0, 2.0, 3.0}mm

### Phase 3: Best Combination
Combine best weight + margin + annealing

## Success Metrics
| Metric | Threshold | Priority |
|--------|-----------|----------|
| Boundary loss | <10.0 | MUST |
| Wirelength ratio | <1.5x baseline | SHOULD |
| Overlap loss | <10.0 | MUST |
| No regression on passing boards | piantor_left, bitaxe_ultra still pass | MUST |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root cause documented (why piantor_right is hard)
- [ ] At least 3 approaches tested with data
- [ ] piantor_right achieves <10.0 boundary loss
- [ ] No regression on currently-passing boards
- [ ] Winning approach documented with config
- [ ] BoundaryLoss updated if code changes needed
- [ ] Test threshold updated from 20.0 → 10.0

BLOCKED BY: temper-x4c (Safety Interlocks)

title: Epic: Hardware Safety Interlock Design and Verification
description:
Design and verify all hardware safety interlocks required for safe induction cooker operation. This epic MUST be completed before any power stage testing.

SAFETY PHILOSOPHY:
Hardware interlocks provide fail-safe protection independent of firmware. Even if the ESP32 crashes, locks up, or has a bug, hardware interlocks must disable the power stage within <1µs.

REQUIRED INTERLOCKS:
1. Overcurrent Protection (OCP) - Trip at 50A peak (125% of rated)
2. Overvoltage Protection (OVP) - Trip at 400V DC bus (110% of nominal)
3. Undervoltage Lockout (UVLO) - Already in UCC21550B, verified in temper-8l2
4. Gate Driver Fault - Fast disable path from comparator to UCC21550 DISABLE pin
5. Thermal Shutdown - Hardware comparator on IGBT heatsink NTC

RESPONSE TIME REQUIREMENT:
- OCP: <1µs from detection to gate driver disable
- OVP: <10µs (slower, bus capacitor provides some hold time)
- Thermal: <100ms (thermal mass provides time)

DESIGN APPROACH:
- Use fast comparators (LM339 or TLV3202 for speed)
- Direct hardware path to UCC21550 DISABLE pin
- OR-gate combining all fault sources
- Latching behavior (requires manual reset after fault)
- LED indicators for fault diagnosis

DELIVERABLES:
1. sim_17_ocp_protection.cir - OCP circuit simulation
2. sim_18_ovp_protection.cir - OVP circuit simulation
3. sim_19_thermal_shutdown.cir - NTC comparator simulation
4. sim_20_interlock_integration.cir - Combined fault logic
5. SAFETY_INTERLOCK_DESIGN.md - Complete design document

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All simulations pass with documented response times
- Response times meet requirements with 2x margin
- Fault conditions tested: overcurrent, overvoltage, overtemp, gate driver UVLO
- Reset and indication logic verified

BLOCKS:
- temper-gqb (Half-Bridge Power Stage) - Cannot test safely without interlocks
- temper-axx (Resonant Tank) - Cannot test safely without interlocks

title: Create current transformer SPICE model
description:
Create a SPICE subcircuit model for the current transformer used in tank current sensing and OCP.

CT REQUIREMENTS:
- Primary: 1 turn (tank wire passes through CT core)
- Secondary: 1000 turns (typical, gives 1000:1 ratio)
- Rated current: 50A primary → 50mA secondary
- Frequency range: 20kHz - 100kHz (induction cooker switching range)
- Accuracy: ±1% for power measurement, ±10% for OCP (conservative trip)

CT MODEL PARAMETERS:
- Turns ratio: N = 1000:1
- Magnetizing inductance: Lm = 10mH (typical for ferrite core)
- Leakage inductance: Ll = 100µH (<<Lm)
- Winding resistance: Rw = 50Ω (secondary)

DELIVERABLES:
- simulation/models/current_transformer.sub
- Validation tests: DC rejection, frequency response, phase accuracy

title: Design overcurrent protection (OCP) comparator circuit
description:
Design hardware OCP circuit using current transformer output to detect overcurrent and disable gate drivers within <1µs.

CIRCUIT REQUIREMENTS:
- Input: CT secondary voltage (proportional to tank current)
- Threshold: 50A peak (set by voltage divider on comparator reference)
- Output: Logic-level signal to fault OR-gate
- Response time: <500ns comparator + <500ns gate driver disable = <1µs total

DESIGN APPROACH:
1. CT burden resistor sized for 50A → ~2.5V
2. Fast comparator (TLV3202: 40ns propagation)
3. Reference voltage from resistor divider
4. Hysteresis resistor to prevent chatter
5. Open-collector output for OR-gate

SIMULATION: sim_17_ocp_protection.cir
DELIVERABLES: OCP circuit, component selection, trip threshold calculation

title: Design overvoltage protection (OVP) comparator circuit
description:
Design hardware OVP circuit to detect DC bus overvoltage (>400V) and disable gate drivers.

CIRCUIT REQUIREMENTS:
- Input: Isolated DC bus voltage sense (resistive divider + optocoupler)
- Threshold: 400V DC (trip point), 380V reset (hysteresis)
- Output: Logic-level signal to fault OR-gate
- Response time: <10µs

ISOLATION APPROACH:
- High-value resistive divider (3x 1MΩ) + optocoupler for isolation
- Divider: 310V → 3.1V using 3MΩ/30kΩ (100:1 ratio)
- Comparator on isolated side for clean logic output

SIMULATION: sim_18_ovp_protection.cir
DELIVERABLES: OVP circuit, isolation boundary documentation

title: Design thermal shutdown comparator circuit
description:
Design hardware thermal protection using NTC thermistor on IGBT heatsink.

CIRCUIT REQUIREMENTS:
- Input: NTC thermistor (10kΩ @ 25°C typical) on IGBT heatsink
- Trip threshold: 85°C (conservative, IGBT rated to 150°C Tj)
- Reset threshold: 75°C (hysteresis prevents cycling)
- Response time: <100ms (thermal mass provides time)

NTC BEHAVIOR:
- At 25°C: 10kΩ, At 85°C: ~2.5kΩ
- Voltage divider with pullup resistor
- Comparator trips when voltage falls below threshold

SIMULATION: sim_19_thermal_shutdown.cir
DELIVERABLES: Thermal circuit, NTC selection, calibration procedure

title: Design fault integration OR-gate and latching logic
description:
Design the fault combination and latching logic that feeds the UCC21550 DISABLE pin.

CIRCUIT REQUIREMENTS:
- Inputs: OCP_FAULT, OVP_FAULT, THERMAL_FAULT, (optional: ESP32_WDT_FAULT)
- Output: GATE_DISABLE signal to UCC21550 DISABLE pin (active HIGH disables)
- Behavior: Latching (any fault latches output HIGH until manual reset)
- Indication: LED per fault source for diagnosis

LATCHING REQUIREMENT:
Once a fault occurs, the system must remain disabled until:
1. Fault condition clears
2. User presses RESET button

IMPLEMENTATION: Comparator with positive feedback (self-latching)

SIMULATION: sim_20_interlock_integration.cir
DELIVERABLES: Fault logic schematic, timing diagram, LED indication

title: Create SAFETY_INTERLOCK_DESIGN.md comprehensive document
description:
Create comprehensive safety interlock design document capturing all hardware protection systems.

DOCUMENT STRUCTURE:
1. Executive Summary - Safety philosophy, response times
2. System Block Diagram - All fault sources, signal flow
3. Overcurrent Protection (OCP) - CT, comparator, timing
4. Overvoltage Protection (OVP) - Sensing, isolation, timing
5. Thermal Protection - NTC, thresholds, placement
6. UVLO - UCC21550B built-in, reference to prior verification
7. Fault Integration Logic - OR-gate, latching, reset
8. Timing Analysis - Worst-case propagation
9. Validation Test Plan - Procedures for each fault type
10. Component BOM - Safety-critical parts list

DELIVERABLES: SAFETY_INTERLOCK_DESIGN.md with all sections

title: Expand PCB Test Suite to 25 Real-World Projects
description:
Expand the external PCB test suite from 5 to 25 projects to provide comprehensive validation coverage for temper-placer optimizer.

## Current Status
- **Existing**: 5 KiCad 6 projects (piantor_left/right, bitaxe_ultra, libresolar_bms, rp2040_designguide)
- **Target**: 25 total projects (20 additional)
- **Coverage**: Much better statistical validation of optimizer quality

## PCB Selection Criteria
1. **License**: Permissive open-source (Apache-2.0, MIT, CERN-OHL-S, etc.)
2. **Format**: KiCad 6 native files (version 20220714+)
3. **Complexity**: Mix of small (10-50), medium (50-150), large (150-300) component counts
4. **Domains**: Diverse application areas (IoT, power electronics, motor control, audio, RF, etc.)
5. **Quality**: Production hardware, not tutorials or experiments
6. **Source**: Established open-source hardware projects

## Target Project Categories
- **Power Electronics**: Motor drivers, buck/boost converters, battery management
- **IoT/Embedded**: ESP32, STM32, Arduino-compatible boards
- **Audio**: Audio interfaces, amplifiers, synthesizers
- **RF/Wireless**: WiFi modules, radio transceivers, antenna designs
- **Industrial**: PLC modules, sensor interfaces, control systems
- **Consumer**: Gaming peripherals, LED controllers, USB devices

## Implementation Steps
1. **Research Phase**: Identify 30-40 candidate projects from GitHub
2. **Screening**: Verify KiCad 6 format, license compatibility, component counts
3. **Download**: Clone repositories and extract PCB files
4. **Processing**: Generate unrouted versions and baseline metrics
5. **Validation**: Ensure all 25 projects parse correctly and have baselines
6. **Testing**: Run optimizer on subset to verify functionality

## Success Criteria
- **25 projects total** with complete test fixtures
- **Balanced complexity**: ~8 small, ~12 medium, ~5 large projects
- **Diverse domains**: At least 6 different application categories
- **All projects pass**: Parser validation and baseline extraction
- **Test coverage**: Optimizer runs successfully on all projects
- **Performance**: Total test suite completes in <30 minutes

## Files to Update
-  - Add new project definitions
-  - Handle larger batch downloads
-  - Update project list
- Documentation: Update test coverage metrics

## Dependencies
- temper-fqt.4 (placement comparison framework) - ✅ COMPLETE
- Existing PCB parsing and baseline extraction pipeline - ✅ COMPLETE

## Estimated Effort
- **Research**: 2-3 hours to identify and screen candidates
- **Download/Processing**: 1-2 hours for automated processing
- **Validation**: 1 hour to verify all projects work
- **Total**: 4-6 hours focused work

## Impact
- **Statistical Significance**: 5x more data points for optimizer validation
- **Real-world Coverage**: Test on diverse PCB design patterns and constraints
- **Quality Assurance**: Better detection of edge cases and failure modes
- **Benchmarking**: Establish robust performance baselines across project types
close_reason: Successfully expanded PCB test suite from 5 to 25 projects across diverse domains including debugging tools, FPGA development, power electronics, SDR hardware, mobile devices, and more. All projects use KiCad 6+ format for compatibility with kiutils. The manifest includes proper categorization, complexity estimates, and comprehensive descriptions for each project.
